fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Montana CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Montana - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity montana, how to transfer CNA license to montana, montana CNA endorsement application

board.name: Montana Nurse Aide Registry
board.parent_dept: Montana Department of Public Health and Human Services (DPHHS)
board.mailing_address: Nurse Aide Registry, Licensure Bureau, P.O. Box 202953, Helena, MT 59620-2953

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Montana.

steps.title: Complete a State-Approved Training Program
steps.description: Successfully complete a Nurse Aide Training Program approved by the Montana Department of Public Health and Human Services (DPHHS).

steps.title: Create an Account in BOUNDS
steps.description: Create an account in the Montana BOUNDS portal and start your Certified Nurse Aide application.

steps.title: Select the Correct Application Path
steps.description: If you are transferring from another state, select 'Certified Nurse Aide' and choose the 'CNA Interstate Endorsement Application' workflow in BOUNDS.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprinting / Background Screening
steps.description: Follow Montana instructions in BOUNDS for the required background screening and submit any fingerprint materials if requested.

steps.title: Track Status and Registry Posting
steps.description: Monitor your application in BOUNDS until the Montana Nurse Aide Registry posts your active status.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does Montana CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: There is no application fee for CNA endorsement in Montana. However, background check fees may apply.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in Montana.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to Montana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Montana while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: Montana endorsement is managed through the BOUNDS portal. Create an account, select Certified Nurse Aide, then choose the CNA Interstate Endorsement Application workflow.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in MT.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Montana.

hero.title: Become a CNA in Montana
hero.dek: To apply for CNA certification in Montana, create an account and submit your application through the BOUNDS portal.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Montana
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Montana CNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Go to https://bounds-mt.powerappsportals.us/.
2. Create a new account and sign in.
3. Select Certified Nurse Aide.
4. Choose CNA Interstate Endorsement Application if you are transferring from another state.
5. Complete each required step and monitor status updates in the portal.
